headline: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5, the first Mythos-class model, anchors this cycle alongside a broad push to make AI agents persist context and run autonomously.
body: The recommended channels lead with Claude Fable 5 (Mythos): Better Stack reports it tops SWE Bench Pro and Frontier Code with vision, long-context, and coding gains [2], while Cyril Imhof covers its use in regulated industries and a "Know Your Agent" approach to context engineering for long-running applications [3]. A second thread is durable agent infrastructure: Better Stack details PAI, a Claude Code Life OS giving agents persistent memory, skills, and a seven-phase workflow [0], and IBM Technology weighs both agent security (prompt injection, self-replicating AI worms, nonhuman identity failures [1]) and the cost, latency, and failure risks of scaling multi-agent systems [5]. Supporting work argues behavior beats size: Snorkel shows a 4B model finetuned with reinforcement learning and tool discipline outperforming Qwen 3 235B [8], Gemma 4's 31B ranks fourth on LM Arena while running on phones and single GPUs under Apache 2.0 [10], and PostHog and Google demo self-correcting agents that turn product signals or validation failures into pull requests and retries [12][13].

cited_items.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
